title: Membranome database

infobox.value: Data about [single-span (bitopic) transmembrane proteins](/wiki/Bitopic_protein "Bitopic protein") in genomes
All bitopic proteins from six model organisms

[Homo sapiens](/wiki/Homo_sapiens "Homo sapiens"), [Arabidopsis thaliana](/wiki/Arabidopsis_thaliana "Arabidopsis thaliana"), [Dictyostelium discoideum](/wiki/Dictyostelium_discoideum "Dictyostelium discoideum"), [Saccharomyces cerevisiae](/wiki/Saccharomyces_cerevisiae "Saccharomyces cerevisiae"), [Escherichia coli](/wiki/Escherichia_coli "Escherichia coli"), [Methanococcus jannaschii](/wiki/Methanococcus_jannaschii "Methanococcus jannaschii")
[University of Michigan College of Pharmacy](/wiki/University_of_Michigan_College_of_Pharmacy "University of Michigan College of Pharmacy")
[PMID](/wiki/PMID_(identifier) "PMID (identifier)") [27510400](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27510400)
<http://membranome.org>
[Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20180716192828/http://membranome.org:80/download.php) 16 July 2018 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine")
[FMAP](https://membranome.org/fmap) and [TMDOCK](https://membranome.org/tmdock)
Curated

provides structural and functional information about more than 6000 [single-pass (bitopic) transmembrane proteins](/wiki/Bitopic_protein "Bitopic protein") from *[Homo sapiens](/wiki/Homo_sapiens "Homo sapiens")*, *[Arabidopsis thaliana](/wiki/Arabidopsis_thaliana "Arabidopsis thaliana")*, *[Dictyostelium discoideum](/wiki/Dictyostelium_discoideum "Dictyostelium discoideum")*, *[Saccharomyces cerevisiae](/wiki/Saccharomyces_cerevisiae "Saccharomyces cerevisiae")*, *[Escherichia coli](/wiki/Escherichia_coli "Escherichia coli")* and *[Methanocaldococcus jannaschii](/wiki/Methanocaldococcus_jannaschii "Methanocaldococcus jannaschii")*.[[1]](#cite_note-Membranome-1-1) Bitopic membrane proteins consist of a single transmembrane alpha-helix connecting water-soluble domains of the protein situated at the opposite sides of a biological membrane. These proteins are frequently involved in the [signal transduction](/wiki/Signal_transduction "Signal transduction") and communication between cells in [multicellular organisms](/wiki/Multicellular_organisms "Multicellular organisms").

(D1): D250–D255. [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.1093/nar/gkw712](https://doi.org/10.1093%2Fnar%2Fgkw712). [PMC](/wiki/PMC_(identifier) "PMC (identifier)") [5210604](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5210604). [PMID](/wiki/PMID_(identifier) "PMID (identifier)") [27510400](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27510400).

*Membranome 2.0: database for proteome-wide profiling of bitopic proteins and their dimers*, by: Lomize, Andrei L., Hage, Jacob M., Pogozheva, Irina D., *[Bioinformatics](/wiki/Bioinformatics_(journal) "Bioinformatics (journal)")*, volume: 34, issue: 6 pages: 1061-1062.

*TMDOCK: An Energy -Based Method for Modeling alpha-Helical Dimers in Membranes*, by Lomize, Andrei L. and Pogozheva, Irina D., [J. Mol. Biol.](/wiki/Journal_of_Molecular_Biology "Journal of Molecular Biology"), Volume: 429 Issue: 3, pages: 390-398
[Dimer verification page of Membranome](https://membranome.org/about#verification)

Lomize, A. L.; Schnitzer, K. A.; Todd, S. C.; Cherepanov, S.; Outeiral, C.; Deane, C. M.; Pogozheva, I. D. (2022). ["Membranome 3.0: Database of single-pass membrane proteins with AlphaFold models"](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9047035). *[Protein Science](/wiki/Protein_Science "Protein Science")*. **31** (5) e4318. [doi](/wiki/Doi_(identifier) "Doi (identifier)"):[10.1002/pro.4318](https://doi.org/10.1002%2Fpro.4318). [PMC](/wiki/PMC_(identifier) "PMC (identifier)") [9047035](https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC9047035). [PMID](/wiki/PMID_(identifier) "PMID (identifier)") [35481632](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35481632).

*Thermodynamic model of secondary structure for alpha-helical peptides and proteins*, by Lomize, AL and Mosberg, HI, [Biopolymers](/wiki/Biopolymers_(journal) "Biopolymers (journal)"), vol 42, issue: 2, pages: 239-269

*Evolution and adaptation of single-pass transmembrane proteins*, by: Pogozheva, Irina D. and Lomize, Andrei L., [Biochimica et. Biophysica Acta Biomembranes](/wiki/Biochimica_et_Biophysica_Acta "Biochimica et Biophysica Acta"), Volume: 1860 Issue: 2 Pages: 364-377

*Membrane proteins structures: A review on computational modeling tools*, by Almeida, Jose G.; Preto, Antonio J., Koukos, Panagiotis I., Bonvin, Alexandre M. J. J., Moreira, Irina S. [Biochimica et. Biophysica Acta](/wiki/Biochimica_et_Biophysica_Acta "Biochimica et Biophysica Acta"), vol. 1859, issue: 10, pages: 2021-2039

*NMR relaxation parameters of methyl groups as a tool to map the interfaces of helix-helix interactions in membrane proteins*, by Lesovoy, D. M., Mineev, K. S., Bragin, P. E., Bocharova, O. V., Bocharov, E. V., Arseniev, A. S., [J. Biomol. NMR](/wiki/Journal_of_Biomolecular_NMR "Journal of Biomolecular NMR"), vol. 69, issue: 3, pages 165-179

*Spatial structure of TLR4 transmembrane domain in bicelles provides the insight into the receptor activation mechanism*, by Mineev, Konstantin S., Goncharuk, Sergey A., Goncharuk, Marina V., Volynsky, Pavel E., Novikova, Ekaterina V., Arseinev, Alexander S., [Scientific Reports](/wiki/Scientific_Reports "Scientific Reports"), vol. 7, article number: 6864

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Online biodatabase of proteins

For other uses, see [Membranome](/wiki/Membranome "Membranome").

Membranome

| Content | |
| --- | --- |
| Description | Data about [single-span (bitopic) transmembrane proteins](/wiki/Bitopic_protein "Bitopic protein") in genomes |
| Data types captured | All bitopic proteins from six model organisms |
| [Organisms](/wiki/Organism "Organism") | [Homo sapiens](/wiki/Homo_sapiens "Homo sapiens"), [Arabidopsis thaliana](/wiki/Arabidopsis_thaliana "Arabidopsis thaliana"), [Dictyostelium discoideum](/wiki/Dictyostelium_discoideum "Dictyostelium discoideum"), [Saccharomyces cerevisiae](/wiki/Saccharomyces_cerevisiae "Saccharomyces cerevisiae"), [Escherichia coli](/wiki/Escherichia_coli "Escherichia coli"), [Methanococcus jannaschii](/wiki/Methanococcus_jannaschii "Methanococcus jannaschii") |
| Contact | |
| [Research center](/wiki/Research_center "Research center") | [University of Michigan College of Pharmacy](/wiki/University_of_Michigan_College_of_Pharmacy "University of Michigan College of Pharmacy") |
| Primary citation | [PMID](/wiki/PMID_(identifier) "PMID (identifier)") [27510400](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27510400) |
| Release date | 2017 |
| Access | |
| Website | <http://membranome.org> |
| Download URL | [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20180716192828/http://membranome.org:80/download.php) 16 July 2018 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine") |
| Tools | |
| [Web](/wiki/Web_application "Web application") | [FMAP](https://membranome.org/fmap) and [TMDOCK](https://membranome.org/tmdock) |
| Miscellaneous | |
| Version | 3.0 |
| Curation policy | Curated |

**Membranome database** provides structural and functional information about more than 6000 [single-pass (bitopic) transmembrane proteins](/wiki/Bitopic_protein "Bitopic protein") from *[Homo sapiens](/wiki/Homo_sapiens "Homo sapiens")*, *[Arabidopsis thaliana](/wiki/Arabidopsis_thaliana "Arabidopsis thaliana")*, *[Dictyostelium discoideum](/wiki/Dictyostelium_discoideum "Dictyostelium discoideum")*, *[Saccharomyces cerevisiae](/wiki/Saccharomyces_cerevisiae "Saccharomyces cerevisiae")*, *[Escherichia coli](/wiki/Escherichia_coli "Escherichia coli")* and *[Methanocaldococcus jannaschii](/wiki/Methanocaldococcus_jannaschii "Methanocaldococcus jannaschii")*.[[1]](#cite_note-Membranome-1-1) Bitopic membrane proteins consist of a single transmembrane alpha-helix connecting water-soluble domains of the protein situated at the opposite sides of a biological membrane. These proteins are frequently involved in the [signal transduction](/wiki/Signal_transduction "Signal transduction") and communication between cells in [multicellular organisms](/wiki/Multicellular_organisms "Multicellular organisms").

The database provides information about the individual proteins including computationally generated three-dimensional models of their transmembrane [alpha-helices](/wiki/Alpha-helices "Alpha-helices") spatially arranged in the membrane, [topology](/wiki/Membrane_topology "Membrane topology"), [intracellular localizations](/wiki/Protein_localization "Protein localization"), [amino acid sequences](/wiki/Amino_acid_sequence "Amino acid sequence"), [domain architecture](/wiki/Protein_domain "Protein domain"), functional annotation and available [experimental structures](/wiki/Protein_structure "Protein structure") from the [Protein Data Bank](/wiki/Protein_Data_Bank "Protein Data Bank"). It also provides a classification of bitopic proteins into 15 functional classes, more than 700 structural superfamilies and 1400 families, along with 3D structures of bitopic protein [complexes](/wiki/Protein_complex "Protein complex") which are also classified to different families.[[1]](#cite_note-Membranome-1-1)
The second Membranome version[[2]](#cite_note-Membranome-2-2) provides 3D models of more than 2000 parallel homodimers formed by TM α-helices of bitopic proteins from different organisms which were generated using TMDOCK program.[[3]](#cite_note-TMDOCK-3) The models of the homodimers were verified through comparison with available experimental data for nearly 600 proteins.[[4]](#cite_note-Verification-4) The database includes downloadable coordinate files of transmembrane helices and their homodimers with calculated membrane boundaries. Membranome 3.0 version incorporates models generated by [AlphaFold 2](/wiki/AlphaFold "AlphaFold").[[5]](#cite_note-5)

The database website provides access to related webservers, FMAP[[6]](#cite_note-Framework-6) and TMDOCK which have been developed for modeling individual alpha-helices and their dimeric complexes in membranes. The database and webservers were used in experimental and bioinformatics studies of bitopic membrane proteins[[7]](#cite_note-Pogozheva-7)[[8]](#cite_note-Almeida-8)[[9]](#cite_note-Lesovoy-9)[[10]](#cite_note-Mineev-10)
